name: The Kansas State Bank Overbrook Kansas
description_short: Kansas State Bank is a community bank in Overbrook, Kansas offering consumer loans, agricultural and business financing, savings accounts, CDs, and online banking services.
description_long: Kansas State Bank operates as a traditional community bank based in Overbrook, Kansas, serving local consumers and agricultural/business customers. The bank is FDIC-insured and promotes itself as a 'hometown bank' focused on personalized service through local relationships. The bank offers a diverse range of financial products including consumer loans for auto purchases and debt consolidation, agricultural and business loans for equipment and facilities, certificate of deposit (CD) accounts with competitive rates, online banking and bill payment, credit cards, and retirement savings services. They also provide educational resources for students on financial literacy. Kansas State Bank distinguishes itself through local community presence and personalized service, emphasizing the advantage of working with a hometown lender rather than national institutions. Their current CD rates (as of February 3, 2026) are competitive within the community bank space, with 12-month CDs at 3.55% APY and declining rates for longer terms. As a smaller regional bank, Kansas State Bank may have more limited digital features and product variety compared to larger national banks, and loan approval processes may vary based on local lending standards. The website provides basic information but limited transparency on fees, rates for specific loan products, or detailed eligibility requirements. Customers seeking specialized financial services beyond traditional banking may need to seek alternative providers.
services: Consumer loans for auto purchases and debt consolidation
Business loans for building purchases and lines of credit
Certificate of Deposit (CD) accounts with tiered rates
Online banking and bill payment services
Credit bureau reporting and loan application processing
pros: FDIC-insured deposits providing federal protection up to $250,000
Competitive CD rates with 3.55% APY on 12-month certificates (effective Feb 2026)
Local community bank with personalized service emphasis and relationship banking
Online banking and bill payment convenience alongside in-person branch service
Agricultural and business loan expertise specific to rural Kansas economy
Educational resources for student financial literacy and consumer awareness
Member FDIC status ensures regulatory oversight and consumer protections
cons: Limited information on consumer loan rates, terms, and approval requirements on website
No details provided on monthly fees for checking/savings accounts or account minimums
Appears to be single-location or small branch network limiting geographic accessibility
Website lacks transparency on credit card offerings, rewards, or APR rates
No mention of mobile app capabilities or mobile banking features
best_for: Rural Kansas residents seeking personalized community banking and local relationship lending
Agricultural businesses and farmers needing specialized ag lending and equipment financing
Consumers wanting competitive CD rates with FDIC safety and local account management
Small business owners looking for relationship-based business loans and lines of credit
diagnosis: Kansas State Bank is best for rural Kansas residents and agricultural/small business customers seeking a community-focused alternative to large national banks with FDIC insurance and relationship-based lending. The main caveat is the lack of pricing transparency on the website—potential customers should contact the bank directly via phone (785-665-7121) to obtain specific rates, terms, and fees before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community bank in Overbrook, Kansas offering consumer and agricultural loans, savings accounts, CDs, and online banking.
